title: apiaceae
intro: The Apiaceae rank, also known as the carrot or parsley family, is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that are primarily herbaceous or woody in nature. Characterized by umbrella-like flower clusters, known as umbels, and often featuring a unique combination of sweet and pungent aromas, this family encompasses a wide range of species. Members of the Apiaceae family are commonly found in a variety of habitats, including meadows, woodlands, and along coastlines. Examples of plants within this family include carrots, parsley, dill, and parsnips, highlighting the family's importance in culinary and medicinal contexts.
